title: peramium schlechtendalianum
intro: Peramium schlechtendalianum, also known as Strychnos nux-vomica, is a plant species within the family Loganiaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant contains a toxic compound called strychnine, which has been used medicinally for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Peramium schlechtendalianum have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other health issues, providing benefits such as pain relief and reduced inflammation. The herbal preparation of the plant is typically made into a powder, capsule, or tincture for oral consumption, with proper caution and under medical guidance due to its toxic nature.
